title: placus membranaceus
side_effects: Placus membranaceus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, or gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and stomach ulcers due to its toxic compounds. Additionally, improper use of the plant may cause respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or asthma attacks in people with pre-existing conditions. In severe cases, misuse of Placus membranaceus can even lead to organ damage, particularly to the liver and kidneys.
